one_time_description: Columbia River Treaty revenue sharing - first time recognition of $9,255,719 with no comparative amount in 2023
economic_notes: aqam operates gas station and convenience store (aqam Trading LP), resources projects including forestry/mineral/gravel/renewable energy (aqam Resources LP), and community economic opportunities (aqam Community Enterprises LP). Investments in Ktunaxa Nation entities including St. Eugene Mission Resort. IMBA and ECDA reserves grew significantly to $46.4M. Specific claims reserve of $7,067,507 established in 2024.
notes: AQAM is a First Nations band in British Columbia (Ktunaxa Nation). The major revenue item is Columbia River Treaty revenue sharing of $9,255,719 which did not exist in prior year - this is a significant one-time or new recurring item that caused total revenue to nearly double. IMBA/ECDA revenues also grew significantly ($12.7M vs $7.1M). The nation has 5 GBEs (aqam Trading Ltd., aqam Resources Ltd., aqam Community Enterprises Ltd., aqam Forestry Ltd., aqam Water Corporation) plus associated limited partnerships, accounted for by modified equity method. No long-term debt. Total government transfers calculated as: Canada $12,366,034 + BC $2,752,528 + FNHA $950,797 + KNC $52,889 + FNGST $272146 + Grants $1,573,018 + Gaming $431,137 + Columbia River Treaty $9,255,719 + Property tax $78,239 = $27,732,507; however property tax and FNGST are own-source; recalculated total_government_transfers excluding property tax and FNGST: $12,366,034+$2,752,528+$950,797+$52,889+$1,573,018+$12,728,558+$431,137+$9,255,719=$40,110,680; OSR = $44,617,832 - $40,110,680 = $4,507,152. Schedule of remuneration not included in extracted text. expenses_infrastructure includes Maintenance of Facilities $1,964,228 + Water System $92,389. expenses_education includes Education $546,805 + Elementary School $1,889,129.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
